channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: These tech leaders have jobs in the Trump administration
item.description: President Trump has been tapping a number of tech executives for a range of roles in his second administration over the past few months. Now, the first week of Trump’s second term, his cabinet is powering through confirmation hearings and unofficial advisors are assuming their posts.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Trump shrugs off billions made through meme coin launch
item.description: President Trump shrugged off the news that he made billions of dollars through the launch of his new meme crypto coin.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: President Donald Trump has pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Domo.AI vision: Agentic workflows that create value
item.description: AI can do much more than make individual employees more productive—with a thoughtful strategy, it can transform your industry. To turn this vision into reality, businesses need AI solutions that are not only ambitious but also practical and adaptable. That’s where Domo steps in. Domo.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: This app locks you out of social media until you go for a walk
item.description: If cutting down on doomscrolling and increasing your step count are on your list of New Year’s resolutions for 2025, then I have the app for you.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok ban on hold for 75 days, but Trump may make a deal with China
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Monday seeking to delay by 75 days the enforcement of a ban of popular short-video app TikTok that was slated to be shuttered on January 19. While signing the order, Trump suggested the United States government should be a half owner of TikTok’s U.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Extreme cold and snow across the South isn't a threat to most native plants and animals, experts say
item.description: A deep freeze and rare snow have brought most things to a standstill from parts of Texas to Florida, but experts say wildlife natives like cypress trees and prairie plants, and cold-blooded gators and snakes, should be alright.
item.dc:creator: Corey Williams, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: X Games to Use AI for Judging Halfpipe Runs
item.description: The X Games will experiment with using artificial intelligence to judge halfpipe runs, a technology that could change the game on subjectively judged sports.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: ServiceOps: Unleashing a new AI agent to reduce change failures in complex systems
item.description: Agentic AI promises to transform enterprise IT work. For CIOs and IT leaders, this means improved operational efficiency, data-driven decision making and accelerated innovation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Samsung and Google's Dolby Atmos rival is coming to Android – but I need it in earbuds before I get excited
item.description: Eclipsa Audio coming to Android is all well and good, but until support for earbuds and headphones lands, I'm on the fence.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: En marcha QDQ-GEO: el primer sistema geoestacionario de distribución de clave cuántica por satélite
item.description: Un paso adelante en materia de seguridad cuántica e innovación tecnológica para el sector aeroespacial.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The iPhone SE 4 could surprisingly get this iPhone 16 feature –and that would make it an even better mid-range option
item.description: The rumors suggest the iPhone SE 4 will have a Dynamic Island like the iPhone 16, as well as matching its internal components.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Build your focus stack with these proven tools and gadgets
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. A dozen tabs open. Notifications pinging. I just opened my laptop and unread emails are already calling my name.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Cloud infrastructure spending more than doubles in the third quarter of 2024
item.description: Spending on compute and storage infrastructure for cloud deployments has surged to unprecedented heights, with 115.3% year-over-year increase in the third quarter of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: From devops to CTO: 5 things to start doing now
item.description: I was promoted to CTO in my late twenties, and while it is common to see young CTOs leading startups these days, it was unusual in the ’90s. I was far less experienced back then, and still developing my business acumen.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Instagram announces new video app amid TikTok troubles
item.description: Instagram announced a new video creation app Sunday amid legal struggles faced in the U.S. by TikTok, another social media platform with a focus on visual content.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Tired of Notion? Sick of Slack? This site will help you find free alternatives
item.description: Let’s be honest: Some apps and services are absolutely worth paying for—but sooner or later, we all reach our limit. With everything from productivity tools to weather apps and even wallpapers shifting to a subscription-style setup, stuff sure can add up fast.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Opinion: Meta’s Trump embrace will have global effects
item.description: Platform's narrower hate-speech policies will have ripple effects in both repressive societies and liberal democracies.
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nossel, The Mercury News

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Supreme Court’s decision on TikTok may have just turned a generation off politics
item.description: Opinions can be shaped very quickly, and grudges last a long time. Donald Trump knows that better than most. Joe Biden ought to, but seemingly doesn’t.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the fate of TikTok hangs in the balance, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu, also called RedNote – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FU TING and DAVID COHEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: These mistakes could tank your credit score
item.description: Even small mistakes can negatively impact your credit score; here are some of the most common credit report errors and how you can fix them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Best ways to give your old iPhone a second life
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the many different ways you can repurpose that old iPhone of yours rather than trashing it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lenovo to acquire Infinidat to expand its storage folio
item.description: Lenovo Group has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Tel Aviv-based enterprise storage provider Infinidat, for an undisclosed sum, to expand its storage offerings. The startup provides several offerings, such as InfiniBox, Infinibox SSA, InfiniGuard, and InfuzeOS.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Federated learning: The killer use case for generative AI
item.description: Let’s imagine a fictional company, Global Retail Corporation, a multinational retail chain struggling with its initial approach to AI integration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Biden’s clean AI infrastructure plan could be hanging by a thread
item.description: An executive order from outgoing US president Joe Biden aimed at advancing the nation’s leadership in AI infrastructure is “welcome and needed in theory, but this one seems rushed and somewhat at odds with the order just passed on AI chip export restrictions,” an analyst said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nile dials-up AI to simplify network provisioning, operation
item.description: Network-as-a-service startup Nile has added an AI-based tool aimed at helping enterprise customers provision and operate the vendor’s Campus Network-as-a-Service deployments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Network convergence will drive enterprise 6G wireless strategies
item.description: The traditional separation between Wi-Fi and cellular networks — a cornerstone of enterprise wireless planning — is facing fundamental challenges according to a new 6G vision statement released this week by the World Broadband Association, “It is important that the 6G era brings together cellular, W
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: From Safety to Accountability: The Game-Changing Impact of Kazakhstan’s Surveillance System
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bill O'Reilly Compares Joe Biden's Departure to Aaron Rodgers' Whimpering
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's farewell address raises concerns about an "oligarchy" of the ultra-wealthy and a 'tech-industrial complex' that threatens democracy, while Bill O'Reilly compares his departure to Aaron Rodgers' struggles with the New York Jets.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation
